threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can tell you're new if you still think the bus will actually arrive on time during a Tube strike. It's like waiting for a kettle to boil.
Last night on the 36, saw a guy arguing with a lamp post like it owed him money. The bus driver just shook his head and muttered 'Another night in Peckham.'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a toddler in a tracksuit trying to negotiate with a stray cat over a half-eaten döner. Classic Neukölln life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a mix of Turkish döner shops and vegan cafes right across the street, both packed at 2 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they dodge the BART delays like it’s a game—everyone else just stands there confused, staring at the screens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog in a tiny blazer on the 14 this morning, looking more put-together than most of the humans. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar has tapas, but some locals still prefer a simple bocadillo over all that fancy fare. We like our sandwiches straightforward.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their fascination with the 'vaca' on Carrer de Verdi—only locals know it’s gone and replaced with a cafe.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: If you’re still using Citymapper for everything and not just for the bus times, it’s obvious you haven’t settled in yet.
Caught a late 36 last week; two lads were arguing about who had the worst haircut. Turns out they were both barbers. Hilarious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I strolled past the closed BVG station on a strike day, saw a street musician jamming with locals, and we all just shrugged—classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a café where the baristas double as local artists, showcasing their latest works on the walls while serving your coffee.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they’ve figured out a way to navigate the late-night taco truck scene without relying on Google Maps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Heard a guy in a parka arguing with a pigeon on the Civic Center platform. Only in SF would the pigeon have the last word.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the vibrant squares, but the real charm hides in the narrow streets where locals share a bottle of vermut on a Tuesday.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stand awkwardly at the metro ticket machines, staring at the options like they're in a spaceship.
